eyebrow: If I Awaken in Los Angeles · Scenic Design
title: Arrival & LED Introduction
summary: Hollywood Boulevard as a wide, shallow stage. The audience stands on the Walk of Fame. The wall is the street. Dancers-as-tourists fill the sidewalk. The tour begins — and then breaks open.
callout:
New layer · dancers as tourists

The sidewalk is alive

The five dancers arrive in Space 1 costumed as tourists — cameras, maps, fanny packs, phones held high. They greet and direct the audience at arrival, move through the sidewalk as Sekou speaks, and after the blackout, return in a line along the sidewalk for the opening dance number. The audience is absorbed into the crowd: we are all tourists here, until the show decides we aren't anymore.

legend.label: Dancers as tourists
legend.note: Roam the sidewalk · line up for the number

beats.title: Arrival
beats.body: Audience enters from the lobby. The five dancers are already in the room, costumed as tourists — cameras around necks, fanny packs, maps, phones up. They warmly greet and direct the audience to find spots along the east wall. The theatrical convention is established without a word: we're all tourists here. Walk of Fame stars glow on the floor. LED wall holds a slow Hollywood Boulevard establishing shot.

beats.title: Sekou enters
beats.body: Spot finds the Tour Guide. "Welcome aboard, folks!" The wall pushes in on TCL Chinese Theatre — pagoda facade, cement handprints. Music: Hollywood Swinging. The tourist-dancers begin moving on the sidewalk — taking selfies, consulting maps, pointing at landmarks on the LED.

beats.title: Landmarks cycle
beats.body: As the Tour Guide narrates, the LED moves through landmark POVs. The tourists react in character — photographing the Hollywood Sign, miming shock at the stars underfoot, posing under Capitol Records, waving a friend into frame. The sidewalk is full of life.

tags.label: DOLBY → WALK OF FAME → CAPITOL → HOLLYWOOD SIGN

beats.title: The pivot
beats.body: Music softens. Tour Guide: "I want you to look deeper." Landmarks recede. The LED lingers on real street-level faces — the fruit vendor, the auditioning woman. The tourists slow their motion. Cameras drop. Maps fold. They start actually looking.

beats.title: The descent
beats.body: "Past the pavement, past the glamour, down into the very soil." LED camera pushes down through the sidewalk. Concrete cracks. Earth. Roots. Walk of Fame stars dim underfoot. The tourists stand still, arrested.

beats.title: Blackout
beats.body: LED wall goes black. Floor goes dark. The room holds a beat of silence. The tour is over.

beats.title: The number
beats.body: Lights rise. The five tourist-dancers are revealed in a line, spread along the sidewalk between the audience and the LED wall — poised, ready. A video plays on the wall; the song plays over the room's speakers. The dancers perform in front of the wall — no live poets, no live band in this space, just video, audio, and movement. The wall is the set; the dancers are the story.

beats.title: Transition to Tunnel
beats.body: The Tour Guide returns. "Alright folks! Follow me…" A lit path on the floor draws the audience out of the room to the left, through a transition tunnel, and into the main show space. The room empties. The LED wall goes dark and stays dark until the audience returns at the very end.

locked: Hollywood Blvd sidewalk setup · audience strung along LED wall
LED POV: across the street · curb + street + opposite sidewalk + landmark
Landmark cycle: TCL → Dolby → Walk of Fame → Capitol → Hollywood Sign
"Look deeper" pivot → camera dives into pavement → soil → roots → blackout
Dancers costumed as tourists · greet and direct audience on arrival
Tourists live on the sidewalk during Sekou's speech — photos, maps, pointing
Post-blackout: dancers revealed in a line on the sidewalk for the number
The number: LED video + song over speakers + dance in front of wall (no live poets, no live band in this space)
No physical scenery — LED + projection + lighting do all the work
Finale reprise: floor stars become real Angeleno names
stillOpen: Song choice for the number — "If I Awaken in LA" or "The Land Remembers Us"
What the video on the wall shows during the number (depends on song)
Tourist costume aesthetic — broad/comedic or contemporary/real?
Do the tourists transform during the dance — shed cameras/maps, become Angelenos — or stay tourists throughout?
LED content during the finale reprise
Full transformed look of Space 1 at show's end
Lighting palette for the Hollywood Blvd cycle — dusk, golden hour, or neon night?
